headline: Ten projects showed public-facing progress this week, anchored by a steady run of educational updates.
subhead: After reviewing the past seven days of repository activity, the diary kept 14 updates worth remembering and set the rest aside.

refreshGate.reason: A forced refresh was requested for the diary archive.

days.label: Today
days.summary: Three projects made the cut, led by interface refinements, new educational material, and visible proof that recent changes actually work.
days.spotlight: Workflow Garden led the day with Build the project content hub and Fix tools tabs overlap proof gap.

entries.title: Workflow Garden turned recent work into proof people can inspect
entries.summary: Interface updates, educational content, and validation checks stood out, especially Build the project content hub and Fix tools tabs overlap proof gap.
entries.narrative: Workflow Garden spent the day building out its content hub, closing a proof gap where tool tabs overlapped, and fixing a mobile hero overflow regression.
entries.whyItMatters: It turns workflow claims into something visitors can inspect instead of simply trust.
entries.exploreNext: Explore Workflow Garden next to see the public context around this work.
notableChanges: Fix tools tabs overlap proof gap
Fix mobile hero overflow proof regression

entries.title: Proof Pack turned recent work into proof people can inspect
entries.summary: Interface and proof work led the way, with clear movement on adding proof screenshots to the readme and fixing wide-screen composition.
entries.narrative: Proof Pack added screenshots to its readme so readers can see the work in action, fixed how the project looks on wide screens, and rebuilt the landing composition with a stricter design gate.
entries.whyItMatters: It turns workflow claims into something visitors can inspect instead of simply trust.
entries.exploreNext: Explore Proof Pack next to see the public context around this work.

fix wide-screen composition in the project
rebuild the landing composition and harden the design gate

days.label: Fri 20 Mar
days.summary: Three projects moved forward, with the clearest signals in educational material, workflow setup, and automation.
days.spotlight: Property Search led the day with : cover crawl smoke registry fallback and : harden crawl smoke source pinning.

entries.title: Property Search made the workflow easier to learn
entries.summary: Educational content, workflow setup, and automation drove the day, especially : cover crawl smoke registry fallback and : harden crawl smoke source pinning.
entries.narrative: Property Search covered a crawl smoke registry fallback, hardened how the smoke source gets pinned, finished selecting a crawl smoke source, and noted an intent to rerun an openreview step.
entries.whyItMatters: It lowers the barrier for someone who is curious about the workflow but not yet fluent in developer tooling.
entries.exploreNext: Explore Property Search next to see the public context around this work.
notableChanges: Finish RAJ-10 crawl smoke source selection

entries.title: Symphony Workspaces made the archive less manual to maintain
entries.summary: Educational content, automation, and testing were the main themes, with clear work on the environment contract and gitignore.
entries.narrative: Symphony Workspaces updated its environment contract, adjusted gitignore, refreshed the repo workflow guide, and revised its ARCHITECTURE doc.
entries.whyItMatters: It makes the archive more dependable and less reliant on manual cleanup or heroic memory.

entries.title: Property Search made the workflow easier to learn

entries.narrative: Property Search aligned its CI pnpm version with the packageManager field, added an initial project scaffold with docs and fixtures, and updated config.toml alongside the environment contract.
